text: What is your most treasured memory?
Describe a time you were offended.
What have you changed your mind about recently?
What's the worst thing someone could say about you? List three things.
What is the greatest accomplishment of your life?
What is a dream you let go of and why?
Describe a time you hurt someone. What happened, and how did you rectify the situation?
Before making a phone call, do you ever rehearse what you're going to say?
What item has the most sentimental value to you?
What is your most terrible memory?
Would you change anything about how you were raised? Why or why not?
What do you find beautiful that others don't?
What insecurity of yours holds you back the most?
What compliment do you think you hear the most, and what compliment do you think I hear the most?
How have you changed in the last few years?
What are you currently unlearning?
How do you talk about people who hurt you? Are you bitter, or do you take accountability?
What scares you beyond the obvious?
How do you show love not just in words but in actions and presence?
What are you proud of and why?
What is your idea of a good life?
What does home feel like to you?
How did your childhood memories shape the way you process emotions and relationships?
Take a deep breath and look into your partner's eyes for four minutes in silence.
Have you ever thought of me as more than a friend?
Share something you consider a positive characteristic of your partner.
What comes first for you: physical or emotional attraction?
If you were to die right now, what would you most regret not telling someone?
What does it mean to be vulnerable with your partner?
What about your life do you wish would never change and why?
In what area of your life do you not trust yourself?
On a scale of 1 to 10, how ready do you feel to be in a relationship?
Say something you are too afraid to say while the other person is wearing headphones.
Pick a song together and dance to it.
Hold hands while telling each other about your first crush.
Share three positive characteristics about your partner.
What was the biggest lesson your past relationship taught you?
Kiss your partner somewhere after asking for consent.
What is something you regret not doing in a past relationship?
How do you think relationships should work?
If you were to marry someone you know now, who would it be?
If someone met you at a party, what misconception might they have about you?
Do you ever feel like you have something to prove?
When was the last time someone told you that you've changed?
What emotion do you feel when walking into your childhood home?
Rate how attractive the person across from you is from 1 to 10.
FaceTime the other person's mom.
Search your name in the other person's messages.
What do you imagine other people assume about you?
Call your mom and tell her about the other person.
What are your top three moments from our friendship?
When do you feel the safest around someone?
Is there a version of you only one person has ever seen?
What small moment changed everything?
What is worse: being unloved or misunderstood?
If your life was a TV show, who would viewers want you to end up with?
If I dared you to do something out of character, what would it be?
What random thing makes you laugh uncontrollably?
If you could live a day in someone else's life, whose would it be?
If we were in a movie together, what scene would be ours?
What is one thing you secretly find irresistible?
What wild impossible thing have you always wanted to do?
If you were a movie character, what would the audience shout at you to do?
Do you think some people are better off never having kids?
What is the kindest lie you have ever told someone you love?
Is closure real or just emotional stalling?
What would your younger self be proud of today?
When did you realize your parents were just people?
What habit would you never admit on a first date?
Would you prefer total transparency or a little mystery?
Do you secretly enjoy red flags until they hurt you?
What do you need during conflict but rarely say out loud?
What did you once think was normal but now see as toxic?
Can people outgrow love? If yes, was it ever real?
How do you handle stress?
How have you shown growth awareness in the past few years?
What do you need inside yourself that you have been neglecting?
How do your fears reveal how safe you feel being seen?
How do you show love consistently through presence?
What are you working toward and why?
What strange belief do you still stand by today?
What would you like people to ask you more about?
Learn one poem a week this year.
Memorize country capitals four per week.
Learn to identify constellations.
Write short reviews of movies or books you experience.
Listen to iconic albums from music history.
Identify plants you see and learn their names.
Learn greetings in several languages.
Draw neurographic lines or mandalas.
Keep a daily thought journal.
Go for a walk and see who gets the most nods from strangers.
Go to an aquarium and name every fish you see.
Decide a new pope between yourselves.
Go to a thrift store and build the best toy army.
Stand guard where one person only tells truths and the other only lies.
Go to Ikea and play family.
Start a flash mob.
Perform a dramatic fake ritual.
Turn your date into a talk show interview.
Return shopping carts in a parking lot competition.
Play 'Will it waffle?' with random foods.
Do a show and tell from your junk drawer.
Go people watching and invent stories.
Plan a fictional bank heist.
Build a Rube Goldberg machine.
Try to beat a high score at an arcade.
Play HORSE while eating donut holes.
Have a lightsaber fight.
Start a podcast debriefing your first date.
Challenge another couple to Four Square.
Test drive a car and see how long you last.
Try solving an old cold case mystery.
Record a dramatic 'if you're watching this I'm dead' video.
Compete to eat the most Costco samples.
Make a bingo card of movie clichés.
Sign up for a welding class and forge rings.
Have a Nerf battle.
